texto: Thousands of years ago, when our ancestors encountered a predatory animal like a lion, it was best to react immediately and not stand around thinking about the lion, admiring its beauty or wondering why it was bothering them instead of tracking down some tasty antelope. Thus, the fast track to the amygdala kept our ancestors alive.
explicacao: Metáfora atribuída a John B. Arden: compara elementos do cotidiano para falar de sentido da existência e experiência vivida. O trecho “Thousands of years ago, when our ancestors encountered a predatory animal like a lion, it was bes…” usa imagem concreta para lembrar que reflexao, fear, fight-or-flight-response pedem movimento e interpretação pessoal, em tom de fight-or-flight-response.

texto: By focusing on possibilities, you can see more than a potential light at the end of the tunnel. The light doesn't have to be at the end of the tunnel; it can illuminate an opportunity wherever you are.
explicacao: Orientação direta de John B. Arden sobre temas ligados a reflexao. A afirmação “By focusing on possibilities, you can see more than a potential light at the end of the tunnel. T…” funciona como lembrete prático ligado a reflexao, opportunity, possibility, útil em momentos de termino e possibility.
